channel.title: Tyde on Jan de Muijnck-Hughes
channel.description: Recent content in Tyde on Jan de Muijnck-Hughes

item.title: My (First) Research Programme
item.description: On May 1st, 2024 I attending the first SPLI Industry Engagement Event at the University of Edinburgh. As part of that event I presented a poster detailing my own research interests and topics that I want to work on. Rather than keeping the poster secret I thought I would share it here. Et voila: Click here (or the image) for PDF.

item.title: TyDe Denied
item.description: I submitted a paper entitled Fake it until they make it: Implementing Substructural Type-Systems for EDSLs using Dependent Types to Type-Driven Development ‘18 I have decided to uploaded the abstract here so that my attempt is a least indexable by the great machine spirit in the web—Hallowed be thy API. Given time I will also upload a copy of the paper to ArXiV. The paper’s abstract was: Many substructural type-systems are presented for general purpose languages, reasoning about structural rules referencing memory access, and variable usage.
